name: HDC-1B "Permafrost"
description: The Permafrost upgrades the single-drill Frostbite to a dual-head configuration mounted on a laterally traversing rail. While one drill string sublimates ice at depth, the other relocates to a fresh borehole, enabling near-continuous extraction without downtime for repositioning. The deeper 5-metre reach accesses ice deposits below the desiccated surface lag layer common at crater rim sites. Both drills share a common capture hood and an enlarged cryocondenser with twice the cooling capacity. Mass rises modestly to 4 tonnes — the traversing rail and second drill string are lightweight — but power doubles to 3.0 MW to supply both heating elements simultaneously. Water output climbs to 208 kg/hr, establishing the Permafrost as the standard early-game ice extraction workhorse for polar crater operations.
